RecipeDialog.CraftTimeReductionTooltip: {initialValue} with {percent}% reduction due to upgrade module {upgradeModuleName}
RecipeDialog.TotalReductionTooltip: with total reduction of {percent}% due to:

ReintegratedToolTip.False: Stop deducting this item from the cost price. This will mathematically increase the price of other products.
ReintegratedToolTip.True: Deduct this item from the cost price, to be used when you can reuse this product in the same recipe or in a previous one (Molds, Barrels, ...). This will mathematically decrease the price of other products.

Share.FirstOnlyTooltip: Grant 100% to first product and zero to the rest. Choose this if you consider other products to be garbage.
Share.HonestTooltip: Grant 80% to first product and split the rest equally.
Share.UnlockTooltip: Disables automatic balancing, allowing manual editing of all values, including going above or below a total of 100%.

ServerDialog.HaveAnInvitation: Do you already have a server join code?

ApiKey.Tooltip: This API Key allows you to contact eco gnome API and edit your server, upload json file, push eco data, ...
ApiKey.Label: Server API Key (secret, do not share)
ApiKey.Refresh: Refresh your API Key in case it has been compromised.
ApiKey.ConfirmRefresh: Are you sure you want to refresh your API Key ? You will need to update any service that use your current API Key.

ModUpload.Description: You are part of a small group of server admins allowed to upload mod icons on Eco Gnome. Simply upload a .unity3d file to import its icons into the site. These icons will be available for all users and all servers. Icons are identified by their item class name (e.g., WorkbenchItem). This feature is resource-intensive, so please use it carefully and avoid spamming. Contact Zangdar on discord if you have any issues or questions.

ServerManagement.JoinCodeTooltip: Give this code to your players so they can join your server.

ServerSettings.Title: Server Settings
ServerSettings.EcoGnomeModExplanation1: Eco Gnome is capable of reading data from your Eco server to accurately calculate the prices of your items. It extracts skills, crafting tables, modules, items, and all recipes. It can read both modified vanilla data and data added by external mods.
ServerSettings.EcoGnomeModExplanation2: To do this, you need to download and install our server mod, [EcoGnomeMod](https://github.com/Eco-Gnome/eco-gnome-mod), on your Eco server. Once your server is restarted, a file named eco_gnome_data.json should be created in the root folder of your game server. You need to retrieve this file and upload it using the button below.

Snackbar.UploadWrongVersion: Incompatible data file version. Supported version: {version}. Please update your EcoGnomeMod.dll on the server to the latest version, then upload the newly generated data file.
Snackbar.UploadPartiallyNok: Warning: {errorCount} elements have not been imported:
Snackbar.UploadPartiallyNokEnd: Review your mods and server edits. You can contact Eco Gnome admins for help.

ShoppingList.ConfirmDeleteShoppingList: Are you sure you want to delete shopping list {name}?
ShoppingList.AutoExpandTooltip: When enabled, recipe expansion reuses the already chosen recipe for the same item in this shopping list and automatically adds child recipes. If no unique prior choice exists, selection stays manual.
ShoppingList.AutoPropagateRecipeChoiceTooltip: When enabled, a manual recipe selection is propagated to all identical ingredients already present in the tree.
ShoppingList.GuidedAutoExpandTooltip: When enabled, clicking an ingredient starts guided expansion to non-craftable resources. If multiple recipes are possible, the run pauses for your choice and resumes automatically.
ShoppingList.StopGuidedAutoExpandTooltip: Stop the current guided auto-expand run and keep all recipes already added.

PriceCalculator.LoadError: An error occurred during the load. You can try to reset all your data to fix the error.

CostMin.Tooltip: The cost related to this crafting table for each minute of craft. Represent the fuel consumption, parts replacement, eventual crafting fees...

MarginPercent.Tooltip: The margin percent that will be applied on your sale price. Check the 'Margin Calculation Type' tooltip option for calculation details.

NonSkilled.Tooltip: Unlock the list of non-skilled recipes in the recipe selector.

LevelAccessible.Tooltip: Only display recipes that are allowed by your skills levels.

ApplyMarginBetweenSkills.Tooltip: When the system retrieve the price of an ingredient, if this ingredient is from an other skill that the current recipe, then it's margin price will be used instead of it's production price. This allows a more fair production price for other citizens that does not have both skills.

Tooltip.2: MarkUp (default): you sum the CostPrice and Margin*CostPrice/100 to get the SalePrice
Tooltip.3: Gross Margin: The Margin percent is the percent of benefit of your SaleCost, that is capped between -99.99% and 99.99%.

CalorieCost.Tooltip: The cost of the food you eat, for 1000 calories spent in a craft. If you buy only salads that brings 700cal for 5$, then Cost per 1000 calories is 1000 * 5 / 700 = 7,14$
CalorieCost.LockedTooltip: This value is locked by your server admin.
CalorieCost.LockedSnackbar: This value is locked by your server admin.
CalorieCost.BoundedSnackbar: Value adjusted to fit server boundaries.

Margin.LockedTooltip: This value is locked by your server admin.
Margin.LockedSnackbar: This value is locked by your server admin.
Margin.BoundedSnackbar: Value adjusted to fit server boundaries.

PriceTooltip.isTagEmptyPrice: This value will be the minimum price of it's related items (if all values are defined !), unless you choose a specific one by clicking a star.
PriceTooltip.isAutoCalculatedPrice: This value is automatically calculated by the system. If it's empty, make sure you set the price of all 'Items to buy. If there are multiple recipes that create this item, you can select the one you want by clicking a star.'
PriceTooltip.isItemEmptyPrice: Set this price to allow calculation of related recipes
PriceTooltip.isMinPrice: This value is set at the minimum price of the server
PriceTooltip.isMaxPrice: This value is set at the maximum price of the server
PriceTooltip.IsMarginPrice: This value is a margin price, because option 'Apply margin between skills' is activated.

PriceCalculator.CancelOverride: Don't override this product price anymore.
PriceCalculator.AddFavoriteItem: Favorite this item, apply its price to the related tag
PriceCalculator.AddOverride: Override this price, considering it's not a product to sell but a product to buy
PriceCalculator.RecipeTooltip: Open recipe popup, it displays details of calculation
PriceCalculator.RecipeTooltipWithShare: Open recipe popup, it gives ability to control the product price repartition and displays details of calculation
PriceCalculator.MarginTooltip: Your Margin configuration does not allow to calculate a Sale Price. Check your Margin Percent and Margin Calculation Type.
PriceCalculator.AddFavoriteRecipe: Favorite this recipe, apply its price to the related product

Snackbar.CantRemoveLastMargin: You can't remove your last margin

PriceCalculatorGuide.Title: Use the price calculator
PriceCalculatorGuide.Intro: This guide explains how to configure Eco Gnome to get consistent sale prices: skills, recipes, buy prices, calorie cost, table costs, margins, primary choices, and reintegration.

Summary.Title: The principle
Summary.Configure: Declare what you can produce: skills, levels, talents, and crafting tables.
Summary.Inputs: Fill what you buy: resources, items, or tags required by your recipes.
Summary.Outputs: Fill what you sell: recipes, primary choices, and margins.
Summary.Advanced: Advanced setup and calculation details: cost split, dynamic values, reintegration, crafted quantity, and price sources.

Context.Title: 1. Choose a context
Context.Body: A context is a saved scenario. Use several contexts to compare professions, margins, levels, or market assumptions without overwriting your main configuration.

Configure.Title: 2. Configure the player
Configure.Body: Add your skills, levels, talents, and tables. Eco Gnome adds related recipes and applies the reductions available on your server.

BuyPrices.Title: 3. Fill buy prices
BuyPrices.Body: Fill the prices of resources, items, or tags you buy. As long as a required price is missing, dependent recipes may stay without a calculated price.

SellPrices.Title: 4. Configure sales
SellPrices.Body: Add the recipes you sell, choose the needed primary options, and apply the right margins. The resulting sale price can then be copied for the game.

Context.Title: Contexts
Context.Body: A context isolates your whole calculator setup: skills, recipes, tables, margins, options, and prices. Changes are saved only for that context.
Context.Example: Example: create one context per profession to fully separate your setups, or a test context to try another case without changing your main configuration.

Calories.Title: Cost per 1000 calories
Calories.Body: Recipe labor is converted into money with this value. If it is too low, you undervalue your labor; if it is too high, every cost price increases.
Calories.Example: Useful formula: food at $5 for 700 calories => 1000 * 5 / 700 = $7.14 per 1000 calories.

CraftingTableCosts.Title: Table cost/min
CraftingTableCosts.Body: Each crafting table can have a cost per minute. Eco Gnome adds this cost to the cost price of recipes crafted on that table, based on their crafting time.
CraftingTableCosts.Example: Example: a table consumes 50 W and the fuel costs $5 for 80,000 W. The cost/min is 50 * 60 * 5 / 80,000 = $0.1875/min, to enter on the table.

Margins.Title: Margins
Margins.Body: A margin turns cost price into sale price. You can create several margins and choose which one applies to each sold product.
Margins.Example: Mark-up: $100 cost + 20% = $120. Gross margin: the percentage represents profit as part of the final price.

Primary.Title: Primary choices
Primary.Body: Primary means priority choice. For a tag, the star forces the item used to represent that tag. For a product, the star forces the recipe used when several recipes produce the same item.
Primary.Example: Example: a tag offers 3 items. Eco Gnome picks the cheapest one only if all 3 have a price. Otherwise, click the star to force the item to use, or fill the missing prices. The same rule applies when several recipes produce the same item.

Reintegration.Title: Reintegration
Reintegration.Body: Reintegration is used when one recipe product is not really sold, but reused or deducted from cost. It moves from product-to-sell behavior to a value included in cost.
Reintegration.Example: Common cases: molds, barrels, containers, or recoverable elements reused in the production chain. Another example: sand can be crafted through mining recipes, but it is often gathered directly. By reintegrating it, it moves from items to sell to items to buy, letting you enter its price manually.

ServerPrices.Title: Server prices
ServerPrices.Body: An admin can define minimum, default, or maximum prices. Eco Gnome applies them to relevant fields and indicates locked or bounded values.
ServerPrices.Example: If a price remains at min or max, check server price rules before changing your setup.

Ingredients.Title: Ingredients
Ingredients.Body: Each ingredient shows quantity, unit price, and total cost. An information icon usually points to the missing price blocking the calculation.

Products.Title: Multiple products
Products.Body: When a recipe produces several items, choose how to split the cost: first product, equal split, quantity split, honest split, or manual input.

DynamicValues.Title: Dynamic values
DynamicValues.Body: Skills, talents, and modules can modify quantities, time, or labor. Secondary values show the result after reductions.

Rounding.Title: Crafted quantity
Rounding.Body: If Eco rounds consumed ingredients according to the crafted quantity, the crafted quantity selector gives a more realistic price.

Mod.Title: Optional Eco Gnome mod
Mod.Body: If the mod is installed on the game server and your Eco Gnome user is linked to that server, you can use your contexts to sync or create your in-game shop offers.

SyncPrices.Title: Sync prices
SyncPrices.Body: Applies buy and sell prices from an Eco Gnome context to the targeted shop, without recreating its whole setup.

CreateShop.Title: Create offers
CreateShop.Body: Automatically creates shop offers from a context, with the option to handle only buy offers or only sell offers.

ServerLink.Title: Link the server
ServerLink.Body: After server registration and player linking, the mod can open Eco Gnome directly on the right server.

Guide.Title: Discover Eco Gnome
Guide.Intro: Eco Gnome brings together tools to configure your Eco server, calculate consistent prices, prepare crafts, analyze the economy, and sync shops with the optional mod.

Start.Title: Where to start?
Start.Configure: Choose or join an Eco Gnome server to load recipes and game data.
Start.Calculate: Create contexts to separate professions, margins, or market assumptions.
Start.Prepare: Use the calculator and shopping list to prepare prices and production chains.
Start.Analyze: Compare server prices with Economy Viewer or sync your shops with the mod.

Seller.Title: Seller
Seller.Body: Start with the price calculator to define costs, margins, and sale prices.

Buyer.Title: Producer
Buyer.Body: Use the shopping list to expand recipes and identify the resources to prepare.

Admin.Title: Server admin
Admin.Body: Use server management to import data, manage members, and define shared price rules.

ModUser.Title: Mod user
ModUser.Body: Link your server and shops to sync prices from your Eco Gnome contexts.

PriceCalculator.Title: Price calculator
PriceCalculator.Body: Configure skills, recipes, buy costs, and margins to get consistent sale prices.

Points.Setup: Setup by context, profession, levels, talents, and tables.
Points.Inputs: Buy prices for required resources, items, or tags.
Points.Outputs: Cost price, margins, primary choices, and sale price.

ShoppingList.Title: Shopping list
ShoppingList.Body: Prepare a full craft by expanding required recipes down to raw resources.

Points.AutoExpand: Automatic or guided expansion of sub-recipes.
Points.Choices: Manual choice when several recipes can produce an ingredient.

EconomyViewer.Title: Economy Viewer
EconomyViewer.Body: Compare prices configured by players and spot differences on the server.

Points.Global: Global view by item, recipe, skill, or player.
Points.Filters: Filters by player, skill, and incomplete rows.

ServerManagement.Title: Server management
ServerManagement.Body: Administer server data, members, and shared price rules.

Points.Data: Import or copy recipe and item data.
Points.Prices: Min, default, max prices, calorie cost, and server margins.

EcoGnomeMod.Title: Eco Gnome mod
EcoGnomeMod.Body: Optional server extension to connect Eco Gnome to the game and automate shops.

Points.Link: Link game server, Eco Gnome server, and player.
Points.Shops: Automatically create offers from a context.

ShoppingListGuide.Title: Use the shopping list
ShoppingListGuide.Intro: The shopping list helps prepare a full craft: products to make, sub-recipes, quantities, modules, talents, and final resources to gather.

Context.Title: 1. Choose a list
Context.Body: Create one list per crafting project to isolate quantities, recipes, and production choices.

Setup.Title: 2. Check setup
Setup.Body: Skills, talents, tables, and modules influence quantities and crafting time.

Recipes.Title: 3. Add recipes
Recipes.Body: Add the products to craft. The tree view shows required ingredients and quantities to produce.

Expand.Title: 4. Expand ingredients
Expand.Body: Use automatic or guided expansion to turn craftable ingredients into sub-recipes down to raw resources.

EconomyViewerGuide.Title: Read Economy Viewer
EconomyViewerGuide.Intro: Economy Viewer is a read-only view that compares prices configured by server players to spot spreads, missing prices, and adjustment opportunities.

ReadOnly.Title: Read-only
ReadOnly.Body: No price or context is modified from this page. It is only used to compare existing data.

Compare.Title: Compare
Compare.Body: Group by item, recipe, skill, or player to identify minimum, average, maximum prices and spreads.

Drilldown.Title: Drill-down
Drilldown.Body: When several players are configured, open the detail view to understand where price differences come from.

ServerManagementGuide.Title: Administer a server
ServerManagementGuide.Intro: Server management centralizes data used by Eco Gnome: recipes, items, tags, members, price policies, and shared constraints.

Data.Title: Server data
Data.Body: Import a data file, copy an existing server, or check recipe, item, table, and module statistics.

PricePolicies.Title: Price rules
PricePolicies.Body: Define minimum, default, or maximum prices, as well as calorie cost and margin policies.

Members.Title: Members
Members.Body: Manage players linked to the server, their admin rights, and access to configuration tools.

EcoGnomeModGuide.Title: Use the Eco Gnome mod
EcoGnomeModGuide.Intro: The Eco Gnome mod is optional. Installed on the game server, it links the server and players to Eco Gnome, then syncs or creates shop offers from a context.

Install.Title: 1. Install the mod
Install.Body: Installation happens on the Eco server. The mod exposes the commands and actions needed for linking.

Link.Title: 2. Link server and player
Link.Body: The server must be registered in Eco Gnome, then the player must be associated with the right account and server.

Context.Title: 3. Choose a context
Context.Body: Synced prices come from an Eco Gnome context. Prepare buy prices, sell prices, primary choices, and margins before syncing.

Sync.Title: 4. Sync shops
Sync.Body: The mod can apply buy prices, sell prices, or create shop offers depending on the need.

EconomyViewer.ServerContextMissing: Please select a server before opening Economy Viewer.
EconomyViewer.ReadOnlyBanner: Economy Viewer is strictly read-only. No server or player setting can be edited from this page.
EconomyViewer.NoGlobalData: No data available for the current filters.

Drilldown.Title: Player Drill-down
Drilldown.NoCraftingTableData: No crafting table configured for this context.
Drilldown.NoComparisonData: No comparison row available for the selected filters.
Drilldown.AtLeastTwoPlayersRequired: At least 2 players are required to open drill-down.

StatusTooltip.Higher: {1} is higher than {0} (computed as Player 2 - Player 1).
StatusTooltip.Lower: {1} is lower than {0} (computed as Player 2 - Player 1).

Errors.Unauthorized: You are not allowed to access this server data.
Errors.LoadGlobal: Failed to load global economy data

Help.Title: How Economy Viewer Works
Help.Line1: Global view aggregates all server contexts except shopping lists.
Help.Line2: Use Group by, Player and Skill filters to narrow the dataset.
Help.Line3: The Name search filters rows immediately in the visible table.
Help.Line4: Open drill-down to compare any two players.
Help.Line5: This page is read-only and never writes server data.
